meta.name: Margherita Pizza
meta.subtitle: Neapolitan-inspired: tomato, fresh mozzarella, basil, olive oil

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Canonical margherita structure with SSA-friendly roles and timing.
Oven-first with optional pizza stone/steel and optional outdoor/high-heat variants.
Dough is same-day or cold-ferment; recipe includes both paths.

yield.notes: Makes 2 x ~12-inch pizzas (8 slices each). Serves 3–4 depending on sides/appetite.

notes: Dough benefits from fermentation time; best planned ahead.
Leftover slices reheat well in skillet or hot oven.
You can par-bake crusts for freezer-friendly make-ahead pizzas.
Balance comes from good tomatoes, proper salt level, and fresh basil at the end.

optional.label: Cast iron skillet
optional.notes: Useful for reheating slices or making pan-style variant.

ingredientGroups.label: Dough
ingredientGroups.notes: 00 flour gives a tender crust; bread flour gives chew; AP works too.

ingredientGroups.label: Sauce + Toppings
ingredientGroups.notes: Keep toppings minimal for margherita: light sauce, fresh mozzarella, basil, olive oil.

ingredients.label: Flour (00, bread, or all-purpose)
notes: 00 flour is classic for Neapolitan-style; bread flour adds chew; AP is fine.

ingredients.label: Warm water
notes: About 65% hydration.
Target ~95–105°F (35–40°C) if using instant yeast for faster rise.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: About 2 tsp Diamond Crystal or ~1 1/2 tsp Morton (by volume varies).

ingredients.label: Instant yeast
notes: About 1 tsp. For cold ferment, you can reduce to ~1–2 g.

ingredients.label: Olive oil (in dough + finishing drizzle)
ingredients.notes: About 2 tbsp total; you may not use all on top.

ingredients.label: Crushed tomatoes (San Marzano-style if possible)
notes: Use crushed or whole peeled tomatoes you crush by hand.
Keep sauce simple—no cooking needed for classic style.

ingredients.label: Fresh mozzarella, sliced and patted dry
notes: Drain and blot well to avoid soggy pizza.
If using low-moisture mozzarella, use ~200 g shredded.

ingredients.label: Fresh basil leaves
ingredients.notes: Add after baking (or last 1 minute) to preserve aroma.

steps.title: Mix dough
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine warm water and yeast; stir briefly.
Add flour and salt. Mix until shaggy.
Add 1 tbsp olive oil and continue mixing until a cohesive dough forms.

steps.title: Knead until smooth and elastic
instructions: Knead by hand 8–10 minutes (or stand mixer 6–8 minutes) until smoother and elastic.
If very sticky, dust with a small amount of flour (avoid adding too much).
checks: Dough stretches without tearing too quickly (windowpane-ish).

steps.title: First rise (choose same-day or cold ferment)
instructions: Lightly oil the bowl, place dough inside, and cover.
Same-day: let rise at room temperature until doubled, about 1.5–2 hours.
Cold ferment (recommended flavor): refrigerate 12–48 hours; bring to room temp 60–90 minutes before shaping.
checks: Dough roughly doubled and shows bubbles on the surface.

steps.title: Preheat oven (stone/steel if using)
instructions: Place pizza stone/steel on the middle or upper-middle rack (if using).
Preheat oven to its hottest setting (500–550°F / 260–288°C).
Let it heat at least 30 minutes after reaching temp for best results.

temperature.notes: Use max oven temp available; longer preheat improves spring.

checks: Stone/steel is fully preheated for crisp bottom.

steps.title: Make simple tomato sauce
instructions: In a bowl, combine crushed tomatoes with salt.
Optionally add a small drizzle of olive oil.
Taste and adjust: it should be bright and lightly salted.

steps.title: Divide and rest dough balls
instructions: Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface.
Divide into 2 equal pieces (~415 g each).
Shape each into a tight ball and let rest covered 15–20 minutes.
checks: Dough relaxes and is easier to stretch after resting.

steps.title: Shape pizza rounds
instructions: On a lightly floured surface, press dough from the center outward, leaving a thicker rim.
Stretch into a ~12-inch round (avoid tearing).
Transfer to a floured peel or parchment (if baking on a sheet).
checks: Center is thin enough to cook through; rim remains thicker for airy crust.

steps.title: Top lightly (margherita style)
instructions: Spread a thin layer of tomato sauce (about 1/3–1/2 cup) leaving the rim uncovered.
Add mozzarella pieces evenly (don’t overload).
Drizzle a little olive oil over the top.
Hold basil for after baking (or add during last minute).
checks: Toppings are sparse enough to avoid sogginess.

steps.title: Bake until blistered and melted
instructions: Slide pizza onto hot stone/steel (or bake on a preheated sheet).
Bake 7–12 minutes depending on oven heat until crust is browned and cheese is melted with some spots.
If needed, rotate halfway for even browning.

steps.title: Finish with basil and serve
instructions: Remove pizza and immediately top with fresh basil leaves.
Optional: drizzle a touch more olive oil and a pinch of salt.
Rest 1–2 minutes, slice, and serve.

steps.title: Repeat with second dough ball
instructions: Shape, top, and bake the second pizza the same way.
If using stone/steel, allow 3–5 minutes between pizzas for surface temp to recover.

notes: Use caution when handling hot stone/steel and when sliding pizza into the oven.
If using fresh mozzarella, keep refrigerated until ready and don’t leave out longer than needed.
Store leftover slices in an airtight container up to 3 days.
Reheat best in a skillet: medium heat 3–5 minutes, cover briefly to melt cheese.
Freeze baked slices up to 2 months; reheat from frozen in a hot oven.
For make-ahead: par-bake crusts 4–5 minutes, cool, wrap, freeze; top and finish-bake later.
Fresh mozzarella releases water—pat dry and don’t overload.
For a hotter top, finish 30–60 seconds under broiler (watch closely).
If you only have a baking sheet, preheat it in the oven to improve bottom browning.
Use only tomatoes + salt (no oil in sauce).
Add basil after bake; use a very light drizzle of oil.
Use 200 g shredded low-moisture mozzarella.
Bake time may be slightly longer; watch browning.
Oil skillet well, press dough in, rest 10 minutes, top and bake.
Bake 12–16 minutes at 500°F; bottom gets crisp.
Warm olive oil with smashed garlic 2–3 minutes; cool slightly; drizzle after baking.
Cold-ferment dough 24–48 hours for best flavor.
Par-bake crusts for freezer-friendly pizza kits.
